name: Peñascos de los Frailes – rodeo al embalse de la Tajera desde El Sotillo
region_intro: Mountain biking around El Sotillo features a landscape characterized by river valleys, hills, and the presence of reservoirs like the Tajera. The terrain offers a mix of challenging climbs and more gentle paths, often winding through natural areas. This region provides varied conditions for mountain bikers, from open stretches to more secluded routes. Best mountain bike trails around El Sotillo The most popular mountain bike trail is Peñascos de los Frailes – rodeo al embalse de la Tajera desde El Sotillo, a 35.2 miles (56.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 5 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive riding around the Tajera reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Riverside Rest Area – Pelegrina Castle loop from Aragosa, a moderate 15.9 miles (25.6 km) path. This route includes scenic sections along the river and views of Pelegrina Castle. Local mountain bikers also love the Barranco del Río Dulce – Riverside Rest Area loop from Torremocha del Campo, a 22.7 miles (36.6 km) trail leading through the Barranco del Río Dulce, often completed in about 3 hours 3 minutes. Mountain biking around El Sotillo is defined by river valleys, reservoir loops, and varied hill climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in El Sotillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 4 reviews. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore El Sotillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around El Sotillo?
A: There are 16 mountain bike trails documented on komoot for the El Sotillo area, offering a variety of options for different skill levels.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain biking in El Sotillo?
A: The mountain bike trails around El Sotillo cater to a range of abilities. You'll find 2 easy routes perfect for beginners, 13 moderate trails, and 1 difficult route for experienced riders seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in El Sotillo?
A: Yes, beginners can enjoy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrecuadrada de los Valles – Recreational Area with Fountain loop from Torremocha del Campo&lt;/a&gt;. This easy trail covers 18.3 miles (29.5 km) and features gentle elevation changes, making it ideal for those new to mountain biking.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for advanced riders in El Sotillo?
A: For advanced riders, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005367514/penascos-de-los-frailes-rodeo-al-embalse-de-la-tajera-desde-el-sotillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñascos de los Frailes – rodeo al embalse de la Tajera desde El Sotillo&lt;/a&gt; offers a difficult 35.2-mile (56.6 km) ride with significant elevation changes, providing a demanding experience around the Tajera reservoir.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the mountain bike trails in El Sotillo?
A: The trails in El Sotillo are characterized by scenic river valleys, rolling hills, and routes that often loop around reservoirs like the Tajera. You'll encounter a mix of open stretches and more secluded paths through natural areas.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, some routes offer views of historical sites and natural features. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10416115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riverside Rest Area – Pelegrina Castle loop from Aragosa&lt;/a&gt; provides scenic sections along the river and views of Pelegrina Castle. You can also find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Torresaviñán Castle&lt;/a&gt; in the wider area.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in El Sotillo?
A: The mountain bike routes in El Sotillo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 4 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain and the scenic beauty of the river valleys and reservoir loops.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that feature reservoir views?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19811842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajera Reservoir loop from Las Inviernas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 21-mile (33.8 km) route that specifically focuses on riding around the Tajera reservoir, offering continuous water views.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that follow river paths?
A: Yes, the area's landscape includes river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31509308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Río Dulce – Riverside Rest Area loop from Torremocha del Campo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 22.7-mile (36.6 km) trail that leads through the Barranco del Río Dulce, providing riverside sections.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in El Sotillo?
A: While specific seasonal conditions can vary, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for mountain biking in this region, with milder temperatures and often vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder conditions.

Q: Are there any facilities or villages near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, you'll find villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5855435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mandayona Village&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4440581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cifuentes&lt;/a&gt; in the vicinity, which may offer amenities. Some routes also pass by designated rest areas.
tips: Built on the bed of the Tajuña river, the Tajera reservoir offers a wide variety of paths and tracks to explore and appreciate the surrounding landscape. Quite isolated from the big cities, it is an ideal place for those who enjoy mtb combining beautiful views and landscapes.

A place not very visited although it is worth doing if you are in the area. Flanked by the Tajera Reservoir, these curious rock formations owe their name to the appearance of being friars peeking out from among the trees.
